description: TickTick API patterns learned from production use. Load on-demand during write operations.

api_behavior.timestamps: TickTick returns +0000 (UTC). The timeZone field indicates the user's intended local zone. Always convert UTC → Berlin before displaying.
api_behavior.batch_update: Use batch_update_tasks for 3+ updates. Returns id2etag (success) and id2error (failures). Zero errors = all persisted.
api_behavior.complete_recurring: complete_task on a recurring task advances to next occurrence — it does NOT delete the task. The returned task shows the next due date.
api_behavior.point_tasks: Set startDate == dueDate for tasks without duration. This avoids rendering duration bars that visually overlap calendar events.
api_behavior.duration_tasks: Set startDate < dueDate only when the task genuinely occupies a time range (e.g., deep work blocks).
api_behavior.recurring_tasks_warning: Do NOT move recurring tasks (Catch up Email, Update To-Do list, review timeline). Changing startDate/dueDate affects ALL future recurrences. Leave them at their original times.
api_behavior.sync_delay: Writes via API may take 30-60s to appear in TickTick desktop/mobile apps. Creates sync faster than updates.
api_behavior.tag_matching: Tags are lowercase strings. Use tags to match tasks to meeting attendees (e.g., tag 'malte' → attendee malte.kalkoffen@endios.de).

write_verification: After batch_update_tasks, wait for user confirmation that changes are visible in TickTick before proceeding. API success does NOT guarantee immediate client sync.
